headline: Facing hard emotions eased the pain
body: In a trial of 230 people with fibromyalgia, a therapy focused on processing emotions outperformed standard coping-skills training.
caption:
What if some persistent pain is the body carrying what the mind has not yet put down?

In a trial of 230 people with fibromyalgia, a therapy that helps people face and express difficult emotions reduced pain more than standard coping-skills training. Processing stress, conflict and old hurt changed how much pain the nervous system produced.

This does not blame anyone for their symptoms. It points to a real lever. The same emotional load can keep the alarm running in chronic pain, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID.

Recovery often means tending to the nervous system, not just the site of pain. Find a practitioner at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#Fibromyalgia #ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #PainScience #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Lumley MA, Schubiner H, et al. (2017), Emotional awareness and expression therapy, cognitive behavioral therapy, and education for fibromyalgia: a cluster-randomized controlled trial, PAIN, 158(12), 2354 to 2363. EAET reduced fibromyalgia pain more than education and outperformed CBT on several measures. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28796118/
